title: The Floquet-Magnus expansion of unbounded operators
authors: Daniel Burgarth, Robin Hillier, Davide Lonigro, Leonhard Richter
abstract: The Floquet-Magnus expansion is a widely used tool to derive effective descriptions of time-periodic quantum systems by approximating their dynamics with a time-independent Hamiltonian. However, its standard formulation is, strictly speaking, restricted to bounded Hamiltonians. In this work, we extend its definition and analysis to a broad class of time-periodic unbounded Hamiltonians. Our approach is based on an a priori distinct nonperturbative framework for the construction of effective Hamiltonians, which we show to reproduce the Floquet-Magnus expansion. A particular strength of our framework is that it allows us to prove that the resulting effective dynamics approximates the original time evolution propagators to arbitrary order in the high-frequency limit without requiring convergence of the Floquet-Magnus expansion, a condition that is already highly restrictive even in the bounded setting. We illustrate the scope of the method on representative models: the quantum Rabi Hamiltonian in the interaction picture, and the periodically driven quantum harmonic oscillator.
summary: This paper extends the Floquet-Magnus expansion from bounded to unbounded operators, allowing for a rigorous treatment of periodically driven quantum systems. It introduces a new construction that provides explicit error bounds in the high-frequency limit even when the expansion series itself does not converge. The method is successfully demonstrated on the quantum Rabi model and the driven harmonic oscillator.
main_problem: The standard Floquet-Magnus expansion is mathematically restricted to bounded Hamiltonians, making it difficult to rigorously treat physically relevant unbounded operators in periodically driven systems.
main_result: The authors develop a nonperturbative framework that extends the Floquet-Magnus expansion to a broad class of unbounded Hamiltonians, providing explicit error bounds that vanish in the high-frequency limit without requiring the convergence of the expansion series.
method: An iterative integration-by-parts technique and a constructive framework based on Kato's theory of evolution equations are used to derive effective Hamiltonians and error estimates.
model_or_system: The framework is applied to the quantum Rabi Hamiltonian in the interaction picture and a periodically driven quantum harmonic oscillator.
key_observables: Unitary propagators and the difference between exact and approximated dynamics.
important_parameters: The driving period (T), the high-frequency limit (T approaching 0), and the energy scale defined by a reference self-adjoint operator (H0).
main_assumptions: The Hamiltonians must satisfy specific regularity and stability properties (Properties A.1-A.9 and C.1-C.7), including relative boundedness and well-defined commutator structures.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of unbounded operators, presents a nonperturbative integration-by-parts framework, establishes theorems for error control and propagator difference, proves the construction reproduces the Floquet-Magnus expansion, and demonstrates the method via the quantum Rabi and driven harmonic oscillator models.
why_interesting: This provides a rigorous mathematical foundation for using effective Hamiltonians in quantum optics and many-body physics, where unbounded operators (like position/momentum or photon number) are standard, and offers improved error scaling in the high-frequency regime.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
